industries.name: Fertilizers & Agriculture
industries.share: ~95% of all potassium use — the dominant application by an enormous margin
uses: Potassium chloride (KCl, muriate of potash, MOP) is the dominant potash fertilizer — mined from underground sylvinite (KCl + NaCl) deposits or surface brines and applied directly or blended into NPK fertilizers
Potassium sulfate (SOP, K₂SO₄) for chloride-sensitive crops: tobacco, potatoes, fruits, vegetables, citrus, and hops — commands a premium over MOP as it avoids chloride phytotoxicity
Potassium nitrate (KNO₃) for drip irrigation fertigation systems and foliar feeding — supplies both potassium and nitrogen without chloride in high-value horticultural crops
Potassium is an essential macronutrient: regulates stomatal opening (water use efficiency), activates 60+ enzymes, controls phloem loading for sugar transport, and improves drought tolerance

Global potash trade is highly concentrated: Canada, Russia, and Belarus historically controlled ~60% of world supply, making potash a geopolitically sensitive commodity (Belarus sanctions and Russia-Ukraine war caused major supply disruptions 2021–2022)

companies.name: Nutrien
companies.use: World's largest potash producer by capacity. Operates six conventional shaft mines in Saskatchewan (Allan, Cory, Lanigan, Patience Lake, Rocanville, Vanscoy). Also world's largest agricultural retail network (Nutrien Ag Solutions).

companies.name: Mosaic Company
companies.use: World's second-largest potash producer. Mines potash in Saskatchewan, New Mexico, and Brazil (Ona project). Distributes MOP and SOP fertilizers globally. Major producer of phosphate fertilizers enabling full NPK supply.

companies.name: Belaruskali
companies.use: World's largest single potash producer by mine output. Operates the Soligorsk mining complex — among the world's largest potash mines. Under Western sanctions since 2021 following political crisis; exports redirected through Russian ports.

companies.name: Uralkali (EuroChem)
companies.use: Russia's largest potash producer. Operates the Berezniki and Solikamsk mines in the Perm region of the Ural Mountains. Sanctions and logistics restrictions following 2022 Ukraine invasion significantly disrupted exports.

companies.name: K+S Group
companies.use: Germany's largest potash producer. Operates underground mines in Germany and the Bethune solution mine in Saskatchewan, Canada. Also world's largest salt producer (Morton Salt).

companies.name: ICL Group
companies.use: Extracts potassium and bromine from Dead Sea brines at Sodom, Israel — the world's lowest-altitude mineral operation. Also produces SOP and specialty potassium fertilizers. Major dead sea potash exporter to Asia.

industries.name: Industrial Chemicals & Manufacturing
uses: Potassium hydroxide (KOH, caustic potash) produced by electrolysis of KCl brine — used to manufacture liquid soaps and detergents, potassium carbonate (K₂CO₃) for glass and welding fluxes, and as an electrolyte in alkaline batteries

Potassium carbonate (K₂CO₃, potash) — historical origin of the word 'potash' from leaching wood ash — used in specialty glass (optical, TV screens), welding fluxes, food additives, and as a CO₂ absorbent
Potassium permanganate (KMnO₄) as oxidizing agent for water treatment, wastewater purification (iron/manganese removal), and organic chemical synthesis
Potassium iodide (KI) for thyroid radiation protection (Chernobyl, Fukushima emergency stockpiles), photographic chemicals, and nutritional iodine supplementation in salt
Potassium nitrate (KNO₃) as oxidizer in black powder (75% KNO₃, 15% charcoal, 10% sulfur) for fireworks, pyrotechnics, and rocket propellant; also for heat treatment salt baths in metallurgy

companies.name: Olin Corporation
companies.use: Produces potassium hydroxide as co-product of potassium chloride electrolysis at chlor-alkali plants. Supplies KOH for detergent, battery, and chemical manufacturing markets.

companies.name: ICL Group (ICL Specialty Fertilizers)
companies.use: World's largest potassium nitrate producer. Manufactures KNO₃ from potassium chloride and nitric acid for use in specialty fertigation fertilizers, industrial heat transfer fluids, and pyrotechnic applications.

companies.name: Kemira
companies.use: Major specialty chemical producer using potassium hydroxide and potassium carbonate in water treatment chemicals, paper processing aids, and oil field chemistry applications.
